note: Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/BallotProblem.lean:345-346 — theorem ballot_problem : ∀ q p, q < p → uniformOn (countedSequence p q) staysPositive = (p - q) / (p + q); docstring 'The ballot problem', file header cites Theorem 30 of the 100 Theorems List (the ballot problem).
verify_note: decl confirmed at BallotProblem.lean:345 with exact claimed statement/docstring; Wikidata Q2253746 label 'Bertrand's ballot theorem', description '(p-q)/(p+q)' probability — verbatim match to the theorem, so exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/SetTheory/Descriptive/Tree.lean:9-27 defines `Descriptive.tree`, trees in the sense of descriptive set theory (finite-sequence trees stable under prefixes); path key 'Descriptive' confirmed under libraries.Mathlib.modules.SetTheory.sub in catalog/data/hierarchy.json (32 decls, sub.Tree). Small but genuine formal home for the field.
verify_note: path exists (32 decls, one file on abstract prefix-closed trees) but is not the field's formal home — Wikidata Q225869 defines descriptive set theory as 'study of well-behaved subsets of Polish spaces', and that content (Borel/analytic sets, Polish spaces) actually lives at Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Polish.lean (25 decls) + Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Constructions/Polish (75 decls), not here; overclaimed 'field' container on a narrow combinatorial tool file.
